# Terraform MongoDB Atlas Project Module for Azure

 [![Latest Release](https://img.shields.io/github/release/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-azurerm-project.svg?style=for-the-badge)](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-azurerm-project/releases/latest) [![Last Updated](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-azurerm-project.svg?style=for-the-badge)](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-azurerm-project/commits)


Terraform module that provisions a MongoDB Atlas project with Azure Resource Manager integration.
It wraps the generic [terraform-module-mongoatlas-project](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-project)
and extends it with Azure Key Vault (KMS) support for encryption at rest, including automated
cloud provider access setup and Atlas service principal key permission management.


---

This project is part of our comprehensive approach towards DevOps Acceleration. 
[<img align="right" title="Share via Email" width="24" height="24" src="https://docs.cloudops.works/images/ionicons/ios-mail.svg"/>][share_email]

It's 100% Open Source and licensed under the [APACHE2](LICENSE).







We have [*lots of terraform modules*][terraform_modules] that are Open Source and we are trying to get them well-maintained!. Check them out!






## Introduction

This module creates and manages a MongoDB Atlas project optionally backed by Azure Key Vault
encryption at rest. When `settings.encryption_at_rest.enabled` is `true`, the module:

- Creates an Azure Key Vault in the specified resource group and region.
- Generates an RSA (or EC) key for Atlas encryption.
- Configures the Atlas cloud provider access setup (Azure application + service principal)
  via the embedded generic module.
- Grants the Atlas service principal the required key permissions automatically — no manual
  Azure AD application registration is needed.
- Passes the Key Vault and key identifiers to the Atlas encryption-at-rest configuration.

The Atlas project name is derived from the organization / environment / spoke hierarchy and
an optional region suffix, following the Cloud Ops Works naming convention. This can be
overridden with an explicit `name` or `name_prefix` variable.

**Required providers:**

| Provider | Version constraint |
|---|---|
| `hashicorp/azurerm` | `~> 4.59` |
| `mongodb/mongodbatlas` | `~> 2.1` |

**Minimum Terraform version:** `>= 1.7`

**Outputs:**

| Output | Description |
|---|---|
| `project_name` | Name of the created Atlas project |
| `project_id` | ID of the created Atlas project |
| `project_creation_timestamp` | Creation timestamp of the Atlas project |
| `project_backup_policy_id` | Backup policy ID (when configured) |
| `project_kms_key_vault_id` | Azure Key Vault resource ID (when encryption enabled) |
| `project_kms_key_vault_name` | Azure Key Vault name (when encryption enabled) |
| `project_kms_key_id` | Azure Key Vault key ID (when encryption enabled) |
| `project_kms_atlas_app_id` | Atlas Azure application ID from cloud provider access setup |
| `project_kms_atlas_service_principal_id` | Atlas Azure service principal object ID |
| `encryption_at_rest_id` | Encryption at rest configuration ID |
| `imported_alert_statement` | Generated import statement for alerts (when generate_import=true) |
| `imported_alert_json` | Generated import JSON for alerts (when generate_import=true) |

## Usage


**IMPORTANT:** The `master` branch is used in `source` just as an example. In your code, do not pin to `master` because there may be breaking changes between releases.
Instead pin to the release tag (e.g. `?ref=vX.Y.Z`) of one of our [latest releases](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-azurerm-project/releases).


### Basic usage — auto-named project, no encryption

```hcl
# terragrunt.hcl
terraform {
  source = "git::https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-azurerm-project.git//?ref=master"
}

locals {
  local_vars = yamldecode(file("inputs.yaml"))
}

include "root" {
  path = find_in_parent_folders()
}

inputs = merge(
  local.local_vars,
  {
    org = {
      organization_name = "myorg"
      organization_unit = "platform"
      environment_name  = "dev"
      environment_type  = "development"
    }
  }
)
```

```yaml
# inputs.yaml
name_prefix: ""
name: ""
organization_name: "MyAtlasOrg"   # (Optional) Atlas org name for lookup
organization_id: ""                # (Optional) Atlas org ID; takes precedence over name
region: "eastus2"                  # (Optional) Azure region; used for KV location
generate_import: false

settings:
  default_alerts_settings: true
  collect_database_specifics_statistics_enabled: true
  data_explorer_enabled: true
  extended_storage_sizes_enabled: false
  performance_advisor_enabled: true
  schema_advisor_enabled: true
  encryption_at_rest:
    enabled: false
```

---

### Usage with Azure Key Vault encryption at rest

```yaml
# inputs.yaml
name_prefix: ""
name: "my-atlas-project"           # (Optional) Explicit project name
organization_name: "MyAtlasOrg"
organization_id: ""
region: "eastus2"
generate_import: false

settings:
  default_alerts_settings: true
  collect_database_specifics_statistics_enabled: true
  data_explorer_enabled: true
  extended_storage_sizes_enabled: false
  performance_advisor_enabled: true
  schema_advisor_enabled: true
  encryption_at_rest:
    enabled: true
    resource_group_name: "rg-mongodb-encryption"  # (Required) Resource group for Key Vault
    key_vault_name: ""                             # (Optional) Auto-generated when empty
    azure_environment: "AZURE"                     # (Optional) "AZURE" | "AZURE_CHINA" | "AZURE_GERMANY" | "AZURE_US_GOVERNMENT"
    soft_delete_retention_days: 7                  # (Optional) 7–90 days
    purge_protection_enabled: true                 # (Optional) Recommended for production
    key_type: "RSA"                                # (Optional) "RSA" | "EC"
    key_size: 2048                                 # (Optional) RSA: 2048 | 3072 | 4096
    key_opts:                                      # (Optional) Allowed key operations
      - decrypt
      - encrypt
      - sign
      - unwrapKey
      - verify
      - wrapKey
```

---

### Variable reference

| Variable | Type | Required | Description | Default |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `name_prefix` | `string` | No | Prefix prepended to auto-generated project name | `""` |
| `name` | `string` | No | Explicit Atlas project name; overrides `name_prefix` | `""` |
| `organization_name` | `string` | No | Atlas organization name for org-ID lookup | `""` |
| `organization_id` | `string` | No | Direct Atlas organization ID; takes precedence over name lookup | `""` |
| `region` | `string` | No | Azure region for Key Vault deployment (e.g. `eastus2`) | `""` |
| `generate_import` | `bool` | No | Generate OpenTofu import blocks for existing Atlas resources | `false` |
| `settings` | `any` | No | Settings object — see sub-keys below | `{}` |

#### `settings` sub-keys

| Key | Type | Required | Description | Default |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `default_alerts_settings` | `bool` | No | Apply Atlas default alert configurations | `true` |
| `collect_database_specifics_statistics_enabled` | `bool` | No | Collect database-specific statistics | `true` |
| `data_explorer_enabled` | `bool` | No | Enable the Atlas Data Explorer UI | `true` |
| `extended_storage_sizes_enabled` | `bool` | No | Enable extended storage sizes for cluster tiers | `false` |
| `performance_advisor_enabled` | `bool` | No | Enable the Atlas Performance Advisor | `true` |
| `schema_advisor_enabled` | `bool` | No | Enable the Atlas Schema Advisor | `true` |
| `backup_compliance` | `object` | No | Backup compliance policy — see upstream module | `{}` |
| `maintenance` | `object` | No | Maintenance window configuration — see upstream module | `{}` |
| `access_list` | `object` | No | IP access list configuration — see upstream module | `{}` |
| `alerts` | `list` | No | Alert configurations list — see upstream module | `[]` |
| `encryption_at_rest.enabled` | `bool` | No | Enable Azure Key Vault encryption at rest | `false` |
| `encryption_at_rest.resource_group_name` | `string` | **Yes** (when enabled) | Azure resource group for the Key Vault | — |
| `encryption_at_rest.key_vault_name` | `string` | No | Key Vault name (max 24 chars); auto-generated when empty | `""` |
| `encryption_at_rest.azure_environment` | `string` | No | Azure cloud environment: `AZURE`, `AZURE_CHINA`, `AZURE_GERMANY`, `AZURE_US_GOVERNMENT` | `"AZURE"` |
| `encryption_at_rest.soft_delete_retention_days` | `number` | No | Soft-delete retention days (7–90) | `7` |
| `encryption_at_rest.purge_protection_enabled` | `bool` | No | Enable purge protection (irreversible during retention period) | `true` |
| `encryption_at_rest.key_type` | `string` | No | Key type: `RSA` or `EC` | `"RSA"` |
| `encryption_at_rest.key_size` | `number` | No | RSA key size in bits: `2048`, `3072`, `4096` (ignored for EC keys) | `2048` |
| `encryption_at_rest.key_opts` | `list(string)` | No | Allowed key operations | `["decrypt","encrypt","sign","unwrapKey","verify","wrapKey"]` |

## Quick Start

1. Ensure Terraform >= 1.7 and Terragrunt are installed.
2. Configure the `azurerm` provider via `ARM_*` environment variables or `az login`.
3. Configure the `mongodbatlas` provider:
   ```sh
   export MONGODB_ATLAS_PUBLIC_KEY="<your-public-key>"
   export MONGODB_ATLAS_PRIVATE_KEY="<your-private-key>"
   ```
4. Copy `.boilerplate/inputs.yaml` to your deployment directory and populate required values.
5. Create a `terragrunt.hcl` referencing this module (see Usage section).
6. Run:
   ```sh
   terragrunt init
   terragrunt plan
   terragrunt apply
   ```


## Examples

#### Minimal example — auto-named project, no encryption

```hcl
# terragrunt.hcl
terraform {
  source = "git::https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-azurerm-project.git//?ref=master"
}

include "root" {
  path = find_in_parent_folders()
}

inputs = {
  organization_name = "MyAtlasOrg"
  region            = "eastus2"
  settings = {
    default_alerts_settings = true
  }
}
```

---

#### Full example — named project with encryption at rest and custom alerts

```hcl
# terragrunt.hcl
terraform {
  source = "git::https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-azurerm-project.git//?ref=master"
}

locals {
  local_vars = yamldecode(file("inputs.yaml"))
}

include "root" {
  path = find_in_parent_folders()
}

inputs = merge(
  local.local_vars,
  {
    org = {
      organization_name = "acmecorp"
      organization_unit = "platform"
      environment_name  = "prod"
      environment_type  = "production"
    }
    extra_tags = {
      CostCenter = "engineering"
      Team       = "platform"
    }
  }
)
```

```yaml
# inputs.yaml
name_prefix: ""
name: "acme-platform-prod"
organization_name: ""
organization_id: "64abc1234def567890abcdef"
region: "eastus2"
generate_import: false

settings:
  default_alerts_settings: true
  collect_database_specifics_statistics_enabled: true
  data_explorer_enabled: true
  extended_storage_sizes_enabled: false
  performance_advisor_enabled: true
  schema_advisor_enabled: true
  encryption_at_rest:
    enabled: true
    resource_group_name: "rg-mongodb-encryption-prod"
    key_vault_name: ""
    azure_environment: "AZURE"
    soft_delete_retention_days: 90
    purge_protection_enabled: true
    key_type: "RSA"
    key_size: 4096
    key_opts:
      - decrypt
      - encrypt
      - sign
      - unwrapKey
      - verify
      - wrapKey
```



## Makefile Targets
```
Available targets:

  help                                Help screen
  help/all                            Display help for all targets
  help/short                          This help short screen
  init/%                              Initialize the project for a specific cloud provider: %S
  lint                                Lint terraform/opentofu code
  tag                                 Tag the current version

```

## Inputs

| Name | Description | Type | Default | Required |
|------|-------------|------|---------|:--------:|
| <a name="input_extra_tags"></a> [extra\_tags](#input\_extra\_tags) | Extra tags to add to the resources | `map(string)` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_generate_import"></a> [generate\_import](#input\_generate\_import) | (Optional) Generate OpenTofu import blocks for existing Atlas resources | `bool` | `false` | no |
| <a name="input_is_hub"></a> [is\_hub](#input\_is\_hub) | Is this a hub or spoke configuration? | `bool` | `false` | no |
| <a name="input_name"></a> [name](#input\_name) | (Optional) Explicit name for the MongoDB Atlas project; overrides name\_prefix | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_name_prefix"></a> [name\_prefix](#input\_name\_prefix) | (Optional) Prefix for the name of the resources | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_org"></a> [org](#input\_org) | Organization details | <pre>object({<br/>    organization_name = string<br/>    organization_unit = string<br/>    environment_type  = string<br/>    environment_name  = string<br/>  })</pre> | n/a | yes |
| <a name="input_organization_id"></a> [organization\_id](#input\_organization\_id) | (Optional) The ID of the MongoDB Atlas organization where the project will be created | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_organization_name"></a> [organization\_name](#input\_organization\_name) | (Optional) The name of the MongoDB Atlas organization where the project will be created | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_region"></a> [region](#input\_region) | Azure Region to deploy resources into. Example: 'eastus2', defaults to empty string as some of the resources may not require region setting. | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_settings"></a> [settings](#input\_settings) | (Optional) Module settings object controlling project features: backup compliance, maintenance window, IP access list, alert configurations, and Azure Key Vault encryption at rest | `any` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_spoke_def"></a> [spoke\_def](#input\_spoke\_def) | Spoke ID Number, must be a 3 digit number | `string` | `"001"` | no |
